articles.title: From Beyond the Gates
articles.author: Lily
articles.complete_text:
From Beyond the Gates. By Lily.

Unearthly prison doors that shut
My soul within your fold,
Both near and far, your leaves unbar,
Death may not always hold.

Lift up your gates, O paradise,
And let my Savior in.
My awful fear for ages here
Pays tribute unto sin.

The light, the light, I see the light —
Come blessed Savior, now,
My soul to save; beneath the wave
My children's children bow.

My children's hearts turn back to me,
I hear their tender cry;
For them my care, my hourly prayer
Ascends with love on high.

O blessed saviors on the Mount,
How sweet your tidings be
For peace awaits within the gates
To crown and comfort me.
articles.notes: This is a complete poem with title, author attribution, and spiritual/religious themes appropriate for the Relief Society Magazine. It has clear poetic structure and is substantial enough to be a standalone piece.

misc_summary: The remaining content consists of legitimate miscellaneous material including: magazine cover/masthead information, table of contents, advertisers' directory with business advertisements, General Board member listings, and the beginning of an already-extracted article about the International Genealogical Congress.
